event: New York Heart Association class III
blood culture positive for methicillin-susceptible Staphylococcus aureus
transesophageal echocardiogram findings: Sievers type I bicuspid aortic valve
2 cm × 2 cm vegetation on ventricular side of aortic leaflet
4 cm × 3 cm vegetation on A2 segment of mitral valve
magnetic resonance imaging of brain: multiple foci of septic emboli
3 cm aneurysm in right pre-central sulcus
urgent cardiac surgery planned within 12 hours of presentation
aortic valve excised via transaortic approach
perforation repaired with bovine pericardial patch
left ventricular cavity irrigated with antibiotic solution
aortomitral area irrigated with antibiotic solution
25-mm Freestyle porcine aortic root prosthesis sized
inverted prosthesis placed in left ventricular outflow tract
prosthetic rim aligned to noncoronary sinus
prosthesis depth secured with 3 commissural 3-0 PROLENE sutures
prosthetic rim sutured to aortic annulus continuously
noncoronary cusp annulus reconstructed with bovine pericardial strip
coronary ostia reimplanted with 5-0 PROLENE sutures
distal graft to ascending aorta anastomosis completed
intraoperative TEE demonstrated normal aortic root prosthesis function
mild central leak in mitral valve
patient separated from cardiopulmonary bypass successfully
uneventful postoperative stay in intensive care unit
microbiology result of excised tissue negative
